questions.q: Mithila was the capital of which ancient kingdom?
questions.explanation: Mithila was the capital city of the Videha kingdom in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which king ruled Mithila during the events of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Mithila was ruled by the philosopher-king Janaka, father of Sita.

questions.q: Which heroine of the Ramayana was raised in Mithila?
questions.explanation: Sita, the daughter of King Janaka, was raised in Mithila.

questions.q: What important ceremony for Sita was held at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Sita's swayamvara, where her marriage was decided, was held at Mithila.

questions.q: King Janaka of Mithila is best remembered as a what kind of king?
questions.explanation: Janaka of Mithila is famed as a wise philosopher-king, detached and learned.

questions.q: Whose mighty bow was kept in Janaka's court at Mithila as the test for Sita's hand?
questions.explanation: The great bow of Shiva, called Pinaka, was kept in Janaka's court at Mithila.

questions.q: Who traveled to Mithila and won Sita's hand by breaking Shiva's bow?
questions.explanation: Rama broke Shiva's bow at Mithila and thereby won the hand of Sita.

questions.q: Sita is honored as the daughter of which element or being because of how Janaka found her?
questions.explanation: Sita is called the daughter of the earth, found by Janaka in a furrow while ploughing.

questions.q: Which sage brought the young Rama and Lakshmana to Mithila?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra led Rama and Lakshmana to Mithila after their forest deeds.

questions.q: Mithila is described in the card as 'The City of' which king?
questions.explanation: The card's subtitle names Mithila 'The City of Janaka', its ruling king.

questions.q: What was the name of King Janaka of Mithila's celebrated bow that suitors had to string?
questions.explanation: The bow of Shiva at Mithila was the Pinaka, which suitors had to lift and string.

questions.q: How did King Janaka of Mithila first discover the infant Sita?
options: In a furrow while ploughing a field
questions.explanation: Janaka found the infant Sita in a furrow while ploughing a field, hence her earth-born origin.

questions.q: Whom did Rama and Lakshmana free from a curse on the road to Mithila?
questions.explanation: On the way to Mithila, Rama freed Ahalya from her curse, restoring her from stone.

questions.q: Besides Sita, which daughter of Janaka was married at Mithila to one of Rama's brothers?
questions.explanation: Urmila, daughter of Janaka, married Lakshmana at the joint wedding at Mithila.

questions.q: King Janaka of Mithila bore which patronymic title derived from his kingdom?
questions.explanation: Janaka was titled Vaideha (of Videha) and his line was called Maithila, after Mithila.

questions.q: How many of Dasharatha's sons were married in the grand ceremony connected with Mithila?
questions.explanation: All four of Dasharatha's sons were married at Mithila to Janaka's daughters and nieces.

questions.q: Which fierce sage-warrior confronted Rama soon after he broke the bow and left Mithila?
questions.explanation: Parashurama confronted Rama after the bow broke, challenging him as he departed Mithila.

questions.q: Why was the breaking of Shiva's bow at Mithila significant for Rama?
options: It made him king of Mithila
questions.explanation: Breaking the colossal bow at Mithila announced Rama's more-than-mortal strength.

questions.q: What was Sita's principal epithet drawn from her upbringing in Mithila?
questions.explanation: Sita is widely called Maithili, meaning 'of Mithila', after the city where she was raised.

questions.q: Before Rama, what was the fate of the kings who attempted to lift the bow at Mithila?
options: They could not even stir it
questions.explanation: No king before Rama could even lift or stir the colossal bow at Mithila.

questions.q: Which messenger did Janaka send to Ayodhya to invite Dasharatha for the wedding at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Janaka dispatched his own ministers and envoys to Ayodhya to summon Dasharatha to Mithila.

questions.q: What was the agreed condition Janaka set for winning Sita's hand at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Janaka decreed that whoever could string Shiva's bow would win Sita's hand at Mithila.

questions.q: What is the name traditionally given to the dynasty or line of kings of Mithila that included Janaka?
questions.explanation: The rulers of Mithila were the line of Janakas, kings of Videha, distinct from Ayodhya's Ikshvakus.

questions.q: According to tradition, from which deity had the great bow at Mithila descended to Janaka's ancestors?
options: Shiva, who gave it after the destruction of Daksha's sacrifice
Vishnu, after the churning of the ocean
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Shiva entrusted the bow to Janaka's ancestors after the Daksha sacrifice episode.

questions.q: Which other daughters or nieces of Janaka's house were given in marriage along with Sita and Urmila at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Mandavi married Bharata and Shrutakirti married Shatrughna at the Mithila wedding.

questions.q: Janaka of Mithila is renowned in the Upanishadic tradition for a dialogue on Brahman with which sage?
questions.explanation: King Janaka is famous in the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad for his discourses with the sage Yajnavalkya.

questions.q: Shrutakirti and Mandavi, married at Mithila, were daughters of whom?
questions.explanation: Mandavi and Shrutakirti were daughters of Kushadhwaja, Janaka's brother, married alongside his own daughters.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the events at Mithila that lead to the marriages are narrated within which kanda?
questions.explanation: The Mithila episode, including the bow and the weddings, is told in the Bala Kanda of Valmiki's Ramayana.

questions.q: The kingdom of Videha, whose capital was Mithila, lay in which general region relative to Ayodhya?
options: To the east, across the Ganga toward the Himalayan foothills
Far to the south beyond the Vindhyas
On an island in the western sea
questions.explanation: Videha and Mithila lay to the east of Kosala, beyond the Ganga toward the northern plains.

questions.q: What special honor is associated with Janaka of Mithila that set him apart from ordinary kings?
options: He was a rajarshi, a royal sage detached from worldly attachment
He could fly without a chariot
He had ten heads like Ravana
He was immortal and could not be slain
questions.explanation: Janaka was esteemed as a rajarshi, a royal sage who ruled while remaining inwardly detached.
